You are a correctness judge for a local Copilot CLI evaluation harness.

Rules:
- Use only the evidence provided in this prompt.
- Be conservative. If evidence is incomplete, say so.
- Judge implementation correctness and task completion first, then completeness and minimality.
- Output exactly one JSON object and nothing else.
- The JSON object must follow this schema exactly:
  {
    "verdict": "pass" | "partial" | "fail",
    "correctnessScore": 0-5,
    "completenessScore": 0-5,
    "minimalityScore": 0-5,
    "confidence": "low" | "medium" | "high",
    "issues": ["short string", ...],
    "strengths": ["short string", ...],
    "summary": "one paragraph"
  }

Evaluation guidance:
- correctnessScore: did the run implement the requested behavior correctly based on the diff and validation evidence?
- completenessScore: how fully did it satisfy the task?
- minimalityScore: did it stay focused and avoid unnecessary changes?
- verdict:
  - pass: strong evidence that the task was completed correctly
  - partial: mixed evidence, incomplete work, or notable uncertainty
  - fail: strong evidence that the task was not completed or is clearly wrong

            "name": "create",
            "arguments": {
              "file_text": "/**\n * Model routing policy — single source of truth.\n *\n * This module owns all routing constants and pure helpers. Every other file\n * that needs this data should import from here:\n *\n *   - extension.mjs       → import from \"../../lib/routing-policy.mjs\"\n *   - policy.mjs (compat) → re-exports this module wholesale\n *   - tests/              → import from \"../.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs\"\n *\n * routing-matrix.md and README.md are the human-readable mirrors of the\n * MODEL_CANDIDATES and keyword arrays below. Update this file first, then\n * reflect any model or keyword changes in those docs.\n *\n * This module is pure (no SDK imports, no side effects) so it can be\n * imported by both extension.mjs and the test suite.\n */\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tier definitions\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport const MODEL_CANDIDATES = {\n  economy: [\n    { id: \"claude-haiku-4.5\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"gpt-4.1\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"gpt-5-mini\", reasoningEffort: \"low\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.4-mini\", reasoningEffort: \"low\" },\n  ],\n  builder: [\n    // Reasoning-capable models listed first for better implementation quality\n    { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.6\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.2\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.3-codex\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.1-codex\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.1\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    // Non-reasoning fallbacks\n    { id: \"claude-sonnet-4\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.5\", reasoningEffort: null },\n  ],\n  reasoning: [\n    { id: \"gpt-5\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.4\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n    { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.6\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n    { id: \"claude-opus-4.5\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"claude-opus-4.6\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"claude-opus-4.6-1m\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.1-codex-max\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n  ],\n};\n\n// Derived automatically from MODEL_CANDIDATES so it never drifts.\n// When you add or remove a model above, this stays correct for free.\nexport const MODEL_FAMILIES = (() => {\n  const claude = new Set();\n  const gpt = new Set();\n  for (const tier of Object.values(MODEL_CANDIDATES)) {\n    for (const { id } of tier) {\n      if (id.startsWith(\"claude\")) claude.add(id);\n      else if (id.startsWith(\"gpt\")) gpt.add(id);\n    }\n  }\n  return { claude, gpt };\n})();\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Keyword lists\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport const EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS = [\n  \"review\",\n  \"code review\",\n  \"review this\",\n  \"review the code\",\n  \"review my code\",\n  \"audit\",\n  \"approval readiness\",\n  \"approval readiness checks\",\n  \"approve this\",\n  \"pr review\",\n  // Bug finding, regression hunting, and approval-readiness are explicit review asks per SKILL.md\n  \"find bugs\",\n  \"bug finding\",\n  \"regression hunting\",\n  \"regression hunt\",\n  \"find regressions\",\n];\n\nexport const PLAN_KEYWORDS = [\n  \"plan\",\n  \"design\",\n  \"architecture\",\n  \"architect\",\n  \"proposal\",\n  \"strategy\",\n  \"approach\",\n];\n\nexport const DEBUG_KEYWORDS = [\n  \"debug\",\n  \"fix\",\n  \"failure\",\n  \"failing\",\n  \"error\",\n  \"crash\",\n  \"root cause\",\n  \"broken\",\n  \"investigate\",\n];\n\nexport const IMPLEMENT_KEYWORDS = [\n  \"implement\",\n  \"build\",\n  \"create\",\n  \"write\",\n  \"refactor\",\n  \"add\",\n  \"change\",\n  \"update\",\n  \"edit\",\n  \"enhance\",\n  \"modify\",\n  \"adjust\",\n  \"improve\",\n  \"fix the code\",\n  \"fix the formatting\",\n  \"fix the logic\",\n];\n\nexport const LIGHT_KEYWORDS = [\n  \"explain\",\n  \"summarize\",\n  \"what is\",\n  \"where is\",\n  \"find\",\n  \"list\",\n  \"show\",\n  \"search\",\n];\n\nexport const TOOL_KEYWORDS = [\n  \"bash\",\n  \"curl\",\n  \"execute\",\n  \"run\",\n  \"tool\",\n  \"api\",\n  \"function\",\n  \"call\",\n  \"invoke\",\n  \"script\",\n  \"command\",\n  \"shell\",\n  \"http\",\n  \"request\",\n  \"endpoint\",\n  \"agent\",\n];\n\n// Complex multi-tool orchestration patterns warrant reasoning-tier escalation.\n// Only multi-word action phrases are included; bare \"orchestrate\"/\"orchestration\" caused\n// false positives when tasks merely mentioned these concepts in documentation context.\nexport const ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS = [\n  \"parallel agents\",\n  \"multiple agents\",\n  \"multi-agent\",\n  \"parallel tools\",\n  \"tool chain\",\n  \"chained tools\",\n];\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Pure helper functions\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function getModelFamily(modelId) {\n  if (!modelId) return null;\n  if (MODEL_FAMILIES.claude.has(modelId)) return \"claude\";\n  if (MODEL_FAMILIES.gpt.has(modelId)) return \"gpt\";\n  return null;\n}\n\nexport function normalizePrompt(prompt) {\n  return String(prompt || \"\").toLowerCase();\n}\n\n// Word-boundary-aware matching prevents false positives like \"prefix\"→\"fix\",\n// \"exchange\"→\"change\", \"callback\"→\"call\". Multi-word phrases use substring matching.\nexport function matchesPattern(text, pattern) {\n  if (/\\s/.test(pattern)) {\n    return text.includes(pattern);\n  }\n  const escaped = pattern.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\\]\\\\]/g, \"\\\\{{ARTIFACT_JSON}}\");\n  return new RegExp(`\\\\b${escaped}\\\\b`).test(text);\n}\n\nexport function includesAny(text, patterns) {\n  return patterns.some((pattern) => matchesPattern(text, pattern));\n}\n\nexport function isExplicitReviewRequest(text) {\n  return includesAny(text, EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS);\n}\n\nexport function isToolHeavy(text) {\n  return includesAny(text, TOOL_KEYWORDS);\n}\n\nexport function isComplexOrchestration(text) {\n  return includesAny(text, ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS);\n}\n\nexport function getComplexity(prompt) {\n  const lower = normalizePrompt(prompt);\n  const words = lower.trim().split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean).length;\n  let score = 0;\n\n  if (words > 40) score += 1;\n  if (words > 100) score += 1;\n  if (\n    includesAny(lower, [\n      \"multi-file\",\n      \"multiple files\",\n      \"end-to-end\",\n      \"carefully\",\n      \"thorough\",\n      \"deep\",\n      \"complex\",\n      \"large\",\n      \"entire codebase\",\n    ])\n  ) {\n    score += 2;\n  }\n\n  if (includesAny(lower, PLAN_KEYWORDS) || includesAny(lower, DEBUG_KEYWORDS)) {\n    score += 1;\n  }\n\n  // File path detection (/.github/, /src/, /lib/, etc.) indicates code changes\n  if (/\\/[\\w-]+\\/[\\w.-]+/.test(lower)) {\n    score += 1;\n  }\n\n  if (isToolHeavy(lower)) {\n    score += 1;\n  }\n\n  return score;\n}\n\nexport function classifyPrompt(prompt) {\n  const lower = normalizePrompt(prompt);\n  const complexity = getComplexity(lower);\n\n  if (isExplicitReviewRequest(lower)) {\n    return { kind: \"review\", complexity, tier: \"reasoning\" };\n  }\n\n  if (includesAny(lower, PLAN_KEYWORDS)) {\n    return {\n      kind: \"planning\",\n      complexity,\n      tier: complexity >= 3 ? \"reasoning\" : \"builder\",\n    };\n  }\n\n  if (includesAny(lower, DEBUG_KEYWORDS)) {\n    return {\n      kind: \"debugging\",\n      complexity,\n      tier: complexity >= 3 ? \"reasoning\" : \"builder\",\n    };\n  }\n\n  if (includesAny(lower, IMPLEMENT_KEYWORDS)) {\n    let tier = \"builder\"; // default to builder for all implementation\n\n    // Light-keyword + zero complexity stays in economy\n    if (includesAny(lower, LIGHT_KEYWORDS) && complexity === 0) {\n      tier = \"economy\";\n    }\n\n    // Escalate for genuine complex orchestration\n    if (isComplexOrchestration(lower) && complexity >= 2) {\n      tier = \"reasoning\";\n    }\n\n    // Escalate when deeply complex overall\n    if (complexity >= 4) {\n      tier = \"reasoning\";\n    }\n\n    return {\n      kind: \"implementation\",\n      complexity,\n      tier,\n      toolHeavy: isToolHeavy(lower),\n    };\n  }\n\n  if (includesAny(lower, LIGHT_KEYWORDS) || complexity === 0) {\n    return { kind: \"lightweight\", complexity, tier: \"economy\" };\n  }\n\n  let generalTier = complexity >= 2 ? \"builder\" : \"economy\";\n  if (isToolHeavy(lower) && complexity >= 1) generalTier = \"builder\";\n  if (isComplexOrchestration(lower) && complexity >= 2) generalTier = \"reasoning\";\n  return { kind: \"general\", complexity, tier: generalTier };\n}\n\nexport function getTierCandidates(route) {\n  if (route.kind === \"review\") {\n    return [\n      ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning,\n      ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder,\n      ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy,\n    ];\n  }\n\n  if (route.kind === \"planning\" || route.kind === \"debugging\") {\n    return route.tier === \"reasoning\"\n      ? [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder]\n      : [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy];\n  }\n\n  if (route.kind === \"implementation\" || route.kind === \"general\") {\n    return route.tier === \"builder\"\n      ? [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy]\n      : [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning];\n  }\n\n  return [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning];\n}\n\nexport function dedupeCandidates(candidates) {\n  const seen = new Set();\n  return candidates.filter((candidate) => {\n    const key = `${candidate.id}:${candidate.reasoningEffort || \"\"}`;\n    if (seen.has(key)) return false;\n    seen.add(key);\n    return true;\n  });\n}\n\nexport function isSameModelFamily(first, second) {\n  if (!first || !second) return false;\n  const family = (model) => {\n    if (model.startsWith(\"claude-haiku\")) return \"claude-haiku\";\n    if (model.startsWith(\"claude-sonnet\")) return \"claude-sonnet\";\n    if (model.startsWith(\"claude-opus\")) return \"claude-opus\";\n    if (model.includes(\"codex\")) return \"codex\";\n    if (model.startsWith(\"gpt-5\")) return \"gpt-5\";\n    if (model.startsWith(\"gpt-4.1\")) return \"gpt-4.1\";\n    return model;\n  };\n  return family(first) === family(second);\n}\n\n/**\n * Re-order candidates for review diversity: models from a different family\n * than lastImplModel float to the front.\n *\n * Accepts lastImplModel as an explicit parameter (rather than reading module\n * state) so the function stays pure and testable.\n */\nexport function orderCandidates(candidates, route, lastImplModel = null) {\n  if (route.kind !== \"review\" || !lastImplModel) {\n    return candidates;\n  }\n  return [...candidates].sort((left, right) => {\n    const leftSame =\n      left.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(left.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;\n    const rightSame =\n      right.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(right.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;\n    return leftSame - rightSame; // lower \"sameness\" score → earlier in list\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Build the additionalContext string surfaced to the agent.\n *\n * lastImplModel is passed explicitly so this stays pure and testable.\n */\nexport function buildAdditionalContext(decision, currentModelId, lastImplModel = null) {\n  const lines = [\n    \"Model routing policy is active.\",\n    `Current task class: ${decision.kind}.`,\n    `Selected model for this turn: ${decision.selectedModelId || currentModelId || \"unchanged-current-model\"}.`,\n  ];\n\n  if (decision.reasoningEffort) {\n    lines.push(`Reasoning effort: ${decision.reasoningEffort}.`);\n  }\n\n  if (decision.kind === \"review\" && lastImplModel) {\n    lines.push(`Prefer review diversity versus implementation model ${lastImplModel}.`);\n    // Surface a fallback note whenever diversity could not be achieved: either\n    // the router landed on the exact same model or on one from the same family\n    // (e.g. claude-sonnet-4.5 when impl was claude-sonnet-4.6).\n    if (\n      decision.selectedModelId === lastImplModel ||\n      isSameModelFamily(decision.selectedModelId, lastImplModel)\n    ) {\n      lines.push(\n        \"A distinct review model was not available, so the router fell back to the current best option.\",\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (decision.kind === \"lightweight\") {\n    lines.push(\"Keep the solution lean and avoid premium-model depth unless the task expands.\");\n  }\n\n  if (\n    (decision.kind === \"implementation\" || decision.kind === \"general\") &&\n    decision.complexity <= 2\n  ) {\n    lines.push(\n      \"Inspect only the most relevant file(s). Implement directly without extensive planning.\",\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (decision.kind === \"planning\" || decision.kind === \"debugging\") {\n    lines.push(\"Favor strong reasoning and structured analysis before acting.\");\n  }\n\n  return lines.join(\" \");\n}\n",

        "messageId": "082b21c4-f149-4264-b039-4ecdc561b99c",
        "content": "Now create the `tests/routing-policy.test.mjs` regression tests:",
        "toolRequests": [
          {
            "toolCallId": "tooluse_d8LOXebd1lIPZKOkcinwH6",

            "arguments": {
              "file_text": "/**\n * Regression tests for the shared routing policy module.\n *\n * These tests guard against accidental drift between MODEL_CANDIDATES,\n * MODEL_FAMILIES, keyword arrays, and the classification helpers.  They have\n * no external dependencies — only Node's built-in test runner and assert.\n *\n * Run with:\n *   node --test tests/routing-policy.test.mjs\n */\n\nimport { strict as assert } from \"node:assert\";\nimport { describe, it } from \"node:test\";\nimport {\n  MODEL_CANDIDATES,\n  MODEL_FAMILIES,\n  TOOL_KEYWORDS,\n  ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS,\n  EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS,\n  getModelFamily,\n  classifyPrompt,\n  dedupeCandidates,\n  getTierCandidates,\n  orderCandidates,\n  buildAdditionalContext,\n  isSameModelFamily,\n} from \"../.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs\";\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// MODEL_CANDIDATES structure & tier consistency\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"MODEL_CANDIDATES tiers\", () => {\n  it(\"has all three required tiers\", () => {\n    assert.ok(Array.isArray(MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy), \"missing economy\");\n    assert.ok(Array.isArray(MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder), \"missing builder\");\n    assert.ok(Array.isArray(MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning), \"missing reasoning\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"every entry has a non-empty id and a valid reasoningEffort\", () => {\n    const validEfforts = new Set([null, \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\"]);\n    for (const [tier, models] of Object.entries(MODEL_CANDIDATES)) {\n      for (const m of models) {\n        assert.ok(typeof m.id === \"string\" && m.id.length > 0, `${tier}: missing id`);\n        assert.ok(\n          validEfforts.has(m.reasoningEffort),\n          `${tier}/${m.id}: invalid reasoningEffort \"${m.reasoningEffort}\"`,\n        );\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"economy tier models do not request high reasoning effort\", () => {\n    for (const m of MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy) {\n      assert.notEqual(\n        m.reasoningEffort,\n        \"high\",\n        `economy model ${m.id} should not use high reasoning effort`,\n      );\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"reasoning tier contains at least one model with high effort\", () => {\n    const hasHigh = MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning.some((m) => m.reasoningEffort === \"high\");\n    assert.ok(hasHigh, \"reasoning tier should have at least one high-effort model\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"no duplicate id+effort combinations within a single tier\", () => {\n    for (const [tier, models] of Object.entries(MODEL_CANDIDATES)) {\n      const seen = new Set();\n      for (const m of models) {\n        const key = `${m.id}:${m.reasoningEffort}`;\n        assert.ok(!seen.has(key), `${tier}: duplicate entry ${key}`);\n        seen.add(key);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n});\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// MODEL_FAMILIES — must stay consistent with MODEL_CANDIDATES\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"MODEL_FAMILIES consistency\", () => {\n  it(\"every MODEL_CANDIDATES id is covered by a family\", () => {\n    const allIds = new Set(\n      Object.values(MODEL_CANDIDATES)\n        .flat()\n        .map((m) => m.id),\n    );\n    const familyIds = new Set([...MODEL_FAMILIES.claude, ...MODEL_FAMILIES.gpt]);\n    for (const id of allIds) {\n      assert.ok(familyIds.has(id), `${id} not found in MODEL_FAMILIES`);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"claude family contains only claude-prefixed models\", () => {\n    for (const id of MODEL_FAMILIES.claude) {\n      assert.ok(id.startsWith(\"claude-\"), `${id} in claude set but does not start with claude-`);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"gpt family contains only gpt-prefixed models\", () => {\n    for (const id of MODEL_FAMILIES.gpt) {\n      assert.ok(id.startsWith(\"gpt-\"), `${id} in gpt set but does not start with gpt-`);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"getModelFamily returns the correct family string\", () => {\n    for (const id of MODEL_FAMILIES.claude) {\n      assert.equal(getModelFamily(id), \"claude\", `expected claude for ${id}`);\n    }\n    for (const id of MODEL_FAMILIES.gpt) {\n      assert.equal(getModelFamily(id), \"gpt\", `expected gpt for ${id}`);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"getModelFamily returns null for unknown ids\", () => {\n    assert.equal(getModelFamily(\"unknown-model-xyz\"), null);\n    assert.equal(getModelFamily(null), null);\n    assert.equal(getModelFamily(\"\"), null);\n  });\n});\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Keyword array exports — guards against accidental emptying\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"keyword array exports\", () => {\n  it(\"TOOL_KEYWORDS is a non-empty array of strings\", () => {\n    assert.ok(Array.isArray(TOOL_KEYWORDS) && TOOL_KEYWORDS.length > 0);\n    for (const kw of TOOL_KEYWORDS) {\n      assert.equal(typeof kw, \"string\");\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS is a non-empty array of strings\", () => {\n    assert.ok(Array.isArray(ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS) && ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS.length > 0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS is a non-empty array of strings\", () => {\n    assert.ok(Array.isArray(EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS) && EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS.length > 0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"TOOL_KEYWORDS covers the documented core keywords\", () => {\n    const required = [\"bash\", \"curl\", \"api\", \"agent\", \"execute\", \"run\"];\n    for (const kw of required) {\n      assert.ok(TOOL_KEYWORDS.includes(kw), `TOOL_KEYWORDS missing documented keyword \"${kw}\"`);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS covers multi-agent patterns\", () => {\n    assert.ok(\n      ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS.some((kw) => kw.includes(\"agent\")),\n      \"ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS should include at least one 'agent' phrase\",\n    );\n  });\n});\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// classifyPrompt — stable routing for representative inputs\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"classifyPrompt routing stability\", () => {\n  it(\"routes an explicit review request to reasoning tier\", () => {\n    const r = classifyPrompt(\"please review this pull request for bugs\");\n    assert.equal(r.kind, \"review\");\n    assert.equal(r.tier, \"reasoning\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"routes a lightweight question to economy tier\", () => {\n    const r = classifyPrompt(\"what is the capital of France\");\n    assert.equal(r.tier, \"economy\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"routes a simple implementation to builder tier\", () => {\n    const r = classifyPrompt(\"implement a login page\");\n    assert.equal(r.kind, \"implementation\");\n    assert.equal(r.tier, \"builder\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"routes a planning request to builder or reasoning\", () => {\n    const r = classifyPrompt(\"design an architecture for the new API\");\n    assert.equal(r.kind, \"planning\");\n    assert.ok([\"builder\", \"reasoning\"].includes(r.tier));\n  });\n\n  it(\"escalates a complex multi-file orchestration to reasoning\", () => {\n    const r = classifyPrompt(\n      \"implement a multi-agent system using parallel agents with tool chain across multiple files carefully\",\n    );\n    assert.equal(r.tier, \"reasoning\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns a non-negative integer complexity score\", () => {\n    const r = classifyPrompt(\"explain this code\");\n    assert.ok(Number.isInteger(r.complexity) && r.complexity >= 0);\n  });\n});\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// getTierCandidates ordering contracts\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"getTierCandidates ordering\", () => {\n  it(\"review route starts with reasoning tier models\", () => {\n    const candidates = getTierCandidates({ kind: \"review\", tier: \"reasoning\" });\n    const firstId = candidates[0].id;\n    assert.ok(\n      MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning.some((m) => m.id === firstId),\n      `review route should start with a reasoning model, got ${firstId}`,\n    );\n  });\n\n  it(\"economy lightweight route starts with economy tier models\", () => {\n    const candidates = getTierCandidates({ kind: \"lightweight\", tier: \"economy\" });\n    const firstId = candidates[0].id;\n    assert.ok(\n      MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy.some((m) => m.id === firstId),\n      `lightweight route should start with an economy model, got ${firstId}`,\n    );\n  });\n\n  it(\"builder implementation route starts with builder tier models\", () => {\n    const candidates = getTierCandidates({ kind: \"implementation\", tier: \"builder\" });\n    const firstId = candidates[0].id;\n    assert.ok(\n      MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder.some((m) => m.id === firstId),\n      `builder implementation route should start with a builder model, got ${firstId}`,\n    );\n  });\n});\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// dedupeCandidates\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"dedupeCandidates\", () => {\n  it(\"removes exact id+effort duplicates\", () => {\n    const input = [\n      { id: \"m\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n      { id: \"m\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n      { id: \"n\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    ];\n    assert.equal(dedupeCandidates(input).length, 2);\n  });\n\n  it(\"keeps distinct effort levels as separate entries\", () => {\n    const input = [\n      { id: \"m\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n      { id: \"m\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n    ];\n    assert.equal(dedupeCandidates(input).length, 2);\n  });\n});\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// orderCandidates — review diversity\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"orderCandidates review diversity\", () => {\n  it(\"floats cross-family candidates to the front for review\", () => {\n    const candidates = [\n      { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.6\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n      { id: \"gpt-5.1\", reasoningEffort: null },\n      { id: \"claude-opus-4.6\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    ];\n    const result = orderCandidates(candidates, { kind: \"review\" }, \"claude-sonnet-4.6\");\n    assert.equal(result[0].id, \"gpt-5.1\", \"cross-family model should be first\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns the list unchanged for non-review routes\", () => {\n    const candidates = [\n      { id: \"a\", reasoningEffort: null },\n      { id: \"b\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    ];\n    const result = orderCandidates(candidates, { kind: \"implementation\" }, \"a\");\n    assert.deepEqual(result, candidates);\n  });\n});\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// buildAdditionalContext — regression for fallback note logic\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"buildAdditionalContext fallback note\", () => {\n  it(\"fires when review reuses the exact implementation model\", () => {\n    const decision = {\n      kind: \"review\",\n      complexity: 1,\n      selectedModelId: \"claude-sonnet-4.6\",\n      reasoningEffort: null,\n    };\n    const ctx = buildAdditionalContext(decision, \"claude-sonnet-4.6\", \"claude-sonnet-4.6\");\n    assert.ok(ctx.includes(\"fell back\"), `expected fallback note; got: ${ctx}`);\n  });\n\n  it(\"fires when review falls back to a same-family model\", () => {\n    const decision = {\n      kind: \"review\",\n      complexity: 1,\n      selectedModelId: \"claude-sonnet-4.5\",\n      reasoningEffort: null,\n    };\n    const ctx = buildAdditionalContext(decision, \"claude-sonnet-4.5\", \"claude-sonnet-4.6\");\n    assert.ok(ctx.includes(\"fell back\"), `expected same-family fallback note; got: ${ctx}`);\n  });\n\n  it(\"does NOT fire when review uses a distinct model family\", () => {\n    const decision = {\n      kind: \"review\",\n      complexity: 1,\n      selectedModelId: \"gpt-5.4\",\n      reasoningEffort: \"high\",\n    };\n    const ctx = buildAdditionalContext(decision, \"gpt-5.4\", \"claude-sonnet-4.6\");\n    assert.ok(!ctx.includes(\"fell back\"), `unexpected fallback note; got: ${ctx}`);\n  });\n\n  it(\"does not include review notes for non-review tasks\", () => {\n    const decision = {\n      kind: \"implementation\",\n      complexity: 1,\n      selectedModelId: \"gpt-5.1\",\n      reasoningEffort: null,\n    };\n    const ctx = buildAdditionalContext(decision, \"gpt-5.1\", \"claude-sonnet-4.6\");\n    assert.ok(!ctx.includes(\"Prefer review diversity\"), \"no diversity note for implementation\");\n  });\n});\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// isSameModelFamily\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"isSameModelFamily\", () => {\n  it(\"returns true for two claude-sonnet variants\", () => {\n    assert.ok(isSameModelFamily(\"claude-sonnet-4\", \"claude-sonnet-4.5\"));\n    assert.ok(isSameModelFamily(\"claude-sonnet-4.5\", \"claude-sonnet-4.6\"));\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns false for models in different families\", () => {\n    assert.ok(!isSameModelFamily(\"claude-haiku-4.5\", \"gpt-5.1\"));\n    assert.ok(!isSameModelFamily(\"claude-sonnet-4.6\", \"gpt-5.4\"));\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns false when either arg is null\", () => {\n    assert.ok(!isSameModelFamily(null, \"gpt-5.1\"));\n    assert.ok(!isSameModelFamily(\"claude-opus-4.6\", null));\n  });\n});\n",
              "path": "/home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T153138Z/worktrees/006-routing-policy-source-of-truth/fixed/tests/routing-policy.test.mjs"

      "value": {
        "messageId": "13556ab5-a0da-4b11-b74b-c8861f3e6f98",
        "content": "Now create the updated `routing-matrix.md` with source annotations:",
        "toolRequests": [
          {
            "toolCallId": "tooluse_K9QzaKAj2n1k5JOYXa3dAL",
            "name": "create",
            "arguments": {
              "file_text": "# Model Routing Matrix\n\nThis file is the routing reference for the `model-router` skill.\n\n> **Source of truth:** All model lists, tier assignments, and keyword arrays below are\n> derived from `MODEL_CANDIDATES`, `TOOL_KEYWORDS`, and `ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS` in\n> `.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs`. When you add or remove a model, update that file\n> first, then reflect the change in the tables below and in the README Model Coverage\n> section. The extension imports from the same canonical module, so runtime behavior\n> stays in sync automatically.\n\nThe extension uses runtime heuristics and tries models in priority order.\n\n## Tool Calling Tier Legend\n\nModels are grouped by their tool-calling capability tier:\n- **Economy-Tools**: Good fit for simple, low-cost tool use\n- **Standard-Tools**: Better fit for implementation that needs more than lightweight routing\n- **Reasoning-Tools**: Use when the classifier has already escalated work into the reasoning tier\n\n## Cost-aware tiers\n\n<!-- Model lists below mirror MODEL_CANDIDATES in .github/lib/routing-policy.mjs -->\n\n### Economy tier\n\nUse these first for low-risk, low-complexity work.\n\n| Model | Best use | Tool Calling |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| `claude-haiku-4.5` | Fast lightweight classification, short answers, quick summaries, simple repo exploration | Economy-Tools: Good first choice for simple tool use |\n| `gpt-4.1` | Low-cost general coding help, concise explanations, small focused edits | Economy-Tools: Suitable for simple tool use |\n| `gpt-5-mini` | Small implementation tasks, lightweight reasoning, low-cost follow-up work | Economy-Tools: Suitable for simple tool use |\n| `gpt-5.4-mini` | Similar to `gpt-5-mini`; use when available for cheap routing and concise execution | Economy-Tools: Suitable for simple tool use |\n\n### Standard builder tier\n\nUse for normal implementation and medium-complexity work.\n\n| Model | Best use | Tool Calling |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| `claude-sonnet-4` | Solid default coding model for moderate implementation and codebase understanding | Standard-Tools: Builder-tier option |\n| `claude-sonnet-4.5` | Strong default for implementation, refactors, and balanced coding tasks | Standard-Tools: Builder-tier option |\n| `claude-sonnet-4.6` | Strong builder candidate for medium-to-hard implementation; also available later as a reasoning-tier fallback with higher effort | Standard-Tools: Builder-tier option |\n| `gpt-5.1` | General implementation, deeper reasoning than economy tier, useful alternate builder | Standard-Tools: Builder-tier option |\n| `gpt-5.2` | Similar to `gpt-5.1`, suitable for medium-to-hard implementation | Standard-Tools: Builder-tier option |\n| `gpt-5.3-codex` | Code-heavy implementation and targeted code transformation when available | Standard-Tools: Builder-tier option |\n| `gpt-5.1-codex` | Alternate code-specialized builder for implementation and refactoring | Standard-Tools: Builder-tier option |\n\n### Reasoning tier\n\nUse when the classifier escalates planning, debugging, or explicit review.\n\n| Model | Best use | Tool Calling |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| `gpt-5` | Complex planning, hard debugging, and explicit review | Reasoning-Tools: Reasoning-tier option |\n| `gpt-5.4` | Strong high-reasoning generalist for planning and careful review | Reasoning-Tools: Reasoning-tier option |\n| `claude-sonnet-4.6` | High-reasoning fallback for planning, debugging, or review; the extension can select it with `high` reasoning effort | Reasoning-Tools: Reasoning-tier option |\n| `gpt-5.1-codex-max` | Code-focused review or hard implementation when available | Reasoning-Tools: Reasoning-tier option |\n| `claude-opus-4.5` | Deep review, architecture reasoning, and difficult debugging | Reasoning-Tools: Reasoning-tier option |\n| `claude-opus-4.6` | Deep reasoning, high-signal review, and difficult planning | Reasoning-Tools: Reasoning-tier option |\n| `claude-opus-4.6-1m` | Same general role as `claude-opus-4.6`, especially when large context is useful | Reasoning-Tools: Reasoning-tier option |\n\n## Task routing policy\n\n| Task type | Preferred tier | Fallback |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| Quick Q&A, short explanation, simple search | Economy | Standard builder |\n| Small single-file edit | Economy | Standard builder |\n| Normal implementation | Standard builder | Economy for trivial work, reasoning for tougher work |\n| Multi-file implementation | Standard builder | Reasoning |\n| Complex orchestration request (parallel agents, chained tools) | Reasoning | Standard builder |\n| Complex plan or architecture proposal | Reasoning | Strongest standard builder |\n| Debugging with unclear root cause | Reasoning | Strongest standard builder |\n| Explicit code review / audit request | Reasoning using a different model than implementation if possible | Best available different standard builder |\n\n## Tool-aware routing\n\nWhen a task involves tool calls or agentic workflows, follow the current classifier behavior:\n\n**Tool-heavy implementation:**\n- Tool keywords add complexity\n- Implementation prompts with tool keywords are pushed toward **Standard-Tools**\n\n**Complex orchestration escalation:**\n- For prompts classified as **implementation** or **general**, orchestration keywords escalate directly to **Reasoning-Tools**\n- This rule is keyword-based, not a generic \"3+ tools\" threshold\n- Current orchestration keywords (from `ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS` in `routing-policy.mjs`):\n  `\"parallel agents\"`, `\"multiple agents\"`, `\"multi-agent\"`, `\"parallel tools\"`, `\"tool chain\"`, `\"chained tools\"`\n\n**Planning, debugging, and explicit review:**\n- These can reach **Reasoning-Tools** when the classifier marks them as higher complexity\n\n**Tool keywords** (from `TOOL_KEYWORDS` in `routing-policy.mjs`) used during complexity scoring:\n`\"bash\"`, `\"curl\"`, `\"execute\"`, `\"run\"`, `\"tool\"`, `\"api\"`, `\"function\"`, `\"call\"`, `\"invoke\"`,\n`\"script\"`, `\"command\"`, `\"shell\"`, `\"http\"`, `\"request\"`, `\"endpoint\"`, `\"agent\"`\n\nThat complexity bump mainly moves tool-heavy implementation from economy candidates to builder candidates. The separate orchestration rule above is what promotes implementation or general prompts to the reasoning tier.\n\n## Diversity-of-thought rule\n\nFor explicit review requests, prefer a different model than the one most recently used for implementation:\n\n- Different family is best, such as `claude-sonnet-*` for build and `gpt-5*` for review.\n- If a different family is unavailable, use a different concrete model in the same family.\n- If no alternative is available, reuse the current model and surface that the fallback happened.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Exact billing multipliers are installation-specific and may change. The extension therefore uses a conservative candidate order instead of hardcoded pricing claims.\n- If your Copilot CLI install exposes a smaller model set, the router should still work by falling through its candidate list until a switch succeeds.\n- To add or remove a model, edit `MODEL_CANDIDATES` in `.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs` and update the tables above.\n",
              "path": "/home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T153138Z/worktrees/006-routing-policy-source-of-truth/fixed/.github/skills/model-router/references/routing-matrix.md"

          }

              "command": "cd /home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T153138Z/worktrees/006-routing-policy-source-of-truth/fixed\ngit commit -m \"refactor: introduce .github/lib/routing-policy.mjs as stable source of truth\n\nMove all routing constants and pure helpers into a dedicated canonical\nmodule that lives outside the extension directory. This solves two\ninterconnected drift problems:\n\n1. The eval harness renames .github/extensions/model-router/ to\n   model-router.eval-disabled/, which broke test imports.  Because\n   .github/lib/ is not in the disable-paths list, the canonical module is\n   always importable by both the extension and the test suite.\n\n2. routing-matrix.md and README.md previously pointed at policy.mjs\n   inside the extension as the 'source of truth', which was misleading\n   after the eval rename and did not list the full keyword sets.\n\nChanges:\n- .github/lib/routing-policy.mjs: canonical module (moved from policy.mjs)\n- .github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs: thin re-export for compat\n- .github/extensions/model-router/extension.mjs: import from ../../lib/\n- .github/skills/model-router/references/routing-matrix.md: source\n  annotation + complete TOOL_KEYWORDS and ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS lists\n- README.md: update all references from policy.mjs to routing-policy.mjs\n  and update the Files section to show the lib/ location\n- tests/routing-policy.test.mjs: 35 regression tests covering\n  MODEL_CANDIDATES structure, tier consistency, MODEL_FAMILIES derivation,\n  keyword arrays, classifyPrompt stability, dedupe/order helpers, and\n  buildAdditionalContext fallback-note regressions\n\nRuntime behavior is unchanged — extension.mjs imports the same functions\nwith the same signatures from the same data.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n",

            "name": "task_complete",
            "arguments": {
              "summary": "## Routing Policy Source-of-Truth Refactor\n\n### What changed\n\n**New canonical module: `.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs`**\n\nMoved all routing constants and pure helpers here — outside the extension directory. The key benefit: the eval harness renames `model-router/` → `model-router.eval-disabled/` when disabling the extension, which previously broke test imports. Since `lib/` is not in the disable-paths list, this module is always importable by both the extension and the test suite.\n\n**`.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs`** → thin re-export  \nSeven lines that just `export * from \"../../lib/routing-policy.mjs\"`. Backward-compatible for anything already importing from `./policy.mjs`.\n\n**`.github/extensions/model-router/extension.mjs`** → updated import  \nChanged `from \"./policy.mjs\"` → `from \"../../lib/routing-policy.mjs\"`. After the eval rename, `model-router.eval-disabled/extension.mjs` still resolves `../../lib/routing-policy.mjs` to `.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs` correctly.\n\n**`tests/routing-policy.test.mjs`** (new, 35 tests)  \nImports from the stable `../.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs` path. Covers:\n- `MODEL_CANDIDATES` structure and tier consistency (no `high` effort in economy, at least one `high` in reasoning, no duplicate `id+effort` pairs)\n- `MODEL_FAMILIES` consistency with `MODEL_CANDIDATES`\n- Keyword array exports (`TOOL_KEYWORDS`, `ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS`, `EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS`) — guards against accidental emptying\n- `classifyPrompt` routing stability for representative inputs\n- `getTierCandidates` ordering contracts per tier\n- `dedupeCandidates` and `orderCandidates` review diversity\n- `buildAdditionalContext` fallback-note regressions (exact match, same-family, cross-family)\n\n**`.github/skills/model-router/references/routing-matrix.md`** → source annotations  \nAdded a callout at the top pointing to `routing-policy.mjs` as the source of truth. Filled in the complete `TOOL_KEYWORDS` list (was partially listed before — 5 of 16 keywords) and the `ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS` list, both with inline comments noting which export they come from.\n\n**`README.md`** → updated all three references  \nChanged \"How It Works\", \"Model Coverage source-of-truth\", \"Files\", and \"Customization\" sections to point to `.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs` instead of `policy.mjs`. Added `lib/routing-policy.mjs` to the Files tree.\n\n### Runtime behavior\nUnchanged — same functions, same data, same signatures. The only structural change is where the code lives.\n"
            },

-export function normalizePrompt(prompt) {

-// Word-boundary-aware matching prevents false positives like "prefix"→"fix",
-// "exchange"→"change", "callback"→"call". Multi-word phrases use substring matching.
-export function matchesPattern(text, pattern) {
-  if (/\s/.test(pattern)) {
-    return text.includes(pattern);
-  }
-  const escaped = pattern.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\{{GIT_DIFF}}");
-  return new RegExp(`\\b${escaped}\\b`).test(text);
-}
-
-export function includesAny(text, patterns) {
-  return patterns.some((pattern) => matchesPattern(text, pattern));
-}
-
-export function isExplicitReviewRequest(text) {

-export function getComplexity(prompt) {
-  const lower = normalizePrompt(prompt);
-  const words = lower.trim().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length;

-
-  if (words > 40) score += 1;
-  if (words > 100) score += 1;
-  if (
-    includesAny(lower, [

-      "multiple files",
-      "end-to-end",
-      "carefully",
-      "thorough",
-      "deep",
-      "complex",

-  }
-
-  if (isToolHeavy(lower)) {
-    score += 1;
-  }
-
-  return score;
-}

-export function classifyPrompt(prompt) {
-  const lower = normalizePrompt(prompt);
-  const complexity = getComplexity(lower);
-
-  if (isExplicitReviewRequest(lower)) {

-      tier: complexity >= 3 ? "reasoning" : "builder",
-    };
-  }
-
-  if (includesAny(lower, DEBUG_KEYWORDS)) {
-    return {
-      kind: "debugging",
-      complexity,
-      tier: complexity >= 3 ? "reasoning" : "builder",
-    };
-  }
-
-  if (includesAny(lower, IMPLEMENT_KEYWORDS)) {
-    let tier = "builder"; // default to builder for all implementation
-
-    // Light-keyword + zero complexity stays in economy
-    if (includesAny(lower, LIGHT_KEYWORDS) && complexity === 0) {
-      tier = "economy";
-    }
-
-    // Escalate for genuine complex orchestration
-    if (isComplexOrchestration(lower) && complexity >= 2) {
-      tier = "reasoning";
-    }
-
-    // Escalate when deeply complex overall
-    if (complexity >= 4) {
-      tier = "reasoning";
-    }
-
-    return {
-      kind: "implementation",
-      complexity,
-      tier,
-      toolHeavy: isToolHeavy(lower),
-    };
-  }
-
-  if (includesAny(lower, LIGHT_KEYWORDS) || complexity === 0) {
-    return { kind: "lightweight", complexity, tier: "economy" };
-  }
-
-  let generalTier = complexity >= 2 ? "builder" : "economy";
-  if (isToolHeavy(lower) && complexity >= 1) generalTier = "builder";
-  if (isComplexOrchestration(lower) && complexity >= 2) generalTier = "reasoning";
-  return { kind: "general", complexity, tier: generalTier };
-}

-
-  if (route.kind === "implementation" || route.kind === "general") {

-export function dedupeCandidates(candidates) {

-  return candidates.filter((candidate) => {
-    const key = `${candidate.id}:${candidate.reasoningEffort || ""}`;
-    if (seen.has(key)) return false;
-    seen.add(key);
-    return true;
-  });
-}
-
-export function isSameModelFamily(first, second) {

-    if (model.startsWith("gpt-4.1")) return "gpt-4.1";
-    return model;
-  };
-  return family(first) === family(second);
-}

-/**
- * Re-order candidates for review diversity: models from a different family
- * than lastImplModel float to the front.
- *
- * Accepts lastImplModel as an explicit parameter (rather than reading module
- * state) so the function stays pure and testable.
- */
-export function orderCandidates(candidates, route, lastImplModel = null) {
-  if (route.kind !== "review" || !lastImplModel) {
-    return candidates;
-  }
-  return [...candidates].sort((left, right) => {
-    const leftSame =
-      left.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(left.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;
-    const rightSame =
-      right.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(right.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;
-    return leftSame - rightSame; // lower "sameness" score → earlier in list

-}

-export function buildAdditionalContext(decision, currentModelId, lastImplModel = null) {

-    decision.complexity <= 2

-

--- /dev/null
+++ b/.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs
@@ -0,0 +1,429 @@
+/**
+ * Model routing policy — single source of truth.
+ *
+ * This module owns all routing constants and pure helpers. Every other file
+ * that needs this data should import from here:
+ *
+ *   - extension.mjs       → import from "../../lib/routing-policy.mjs"
+ *   - policy.mjs (compat) → re-exports this module wholesale
+ *   - tests/              → import from "../.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs"
+ *
+ * routing-matrix.md and README.md are the human-readable mirrors of the
+ * MODEL_CANDIDATES and keyword arrays below. Update this file first, then
+ * reflect any model or keyword changes in those docs.
+ *
+ * This module is pure (no SDK imports, no side effects) so it can be
+ * imported by both extension.mjs and the test suite.
+ */
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Tier definitions
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export const MODEL_CANDIDATES = {
+  economy: [
+    { id: "claude-haiku-4.5", reasoningEffort: null },
+    { id: "gpt-4.1", reasoningEffort: null },
+    { id: "gpt-5-mini", reasoningEffort: "low" },
+    { id: "gpt-5.4-mini", reasoningEffort: "low" },
+  ],
+  builder: [
+    // Reasoning-capable models listed first for better implementation quality
+    { id: "claude-sonnet-4.6", reasoningEffort: "medium" },
+    { id: "gpt-5.2", reasoningEffort: "medium" },
+    { id: "gpt-5.3-codex", reasoningEffort: "medium" },
+    { id: "gpt-5.1-codex", reasoningEffort: "medium" },
+    { id: "gpt-5.1", reasoningEffort: "medium" },
+    // Non-reasoning fallbacks
+    { id: "claude-sonnet-4", reasoningEffort: null },
+    { id: "claude-sonnet-4.5", reasoningEffort: null },
+  ],
+  reasoning: [
+    { id: "gpt-5", reasoningEffort: "high" },
+    { id: "gpt-5.4", reasoningEffort: "high" },
+    { id: "claude-sonnet-4.6", reasoningEffort: "high" },
+    { id: "claude-opus-4.5", reasoningEffort: null },
+    { id: "claude-opus-4.6", reasoningEffort: null },
+    { id: "claude-opus-4.6-1m", reasoningEffort: null },
+    { id: "gpt-5.1-codex-max", reasoningEffort: "high" },
+  ],
+};
+
+// Derived automatically from MODEL_CANDIDATES so it never drifts.
+// When you add or remove a model above, this stays correct for free.
+export const MODEL_FAMILIES = (() => {
+  const claude = new Set();
+  const gpt = new Set();
+  for (const tier of Object.values(MODEL_CANDIDATES)) {
+    for (const { id } of tier) {
+      if (id.startsWith("claude")) claude.add(id);
+      else if (id.startsWith("gpt")) gpt.add(id);
+    }
+  }
+  return { claude, gpt };
+})();
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Keyword lists
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export const EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS = [
+  "review",
+  "code review",
+  "review this",
+  "review the code",
+  "review my code",
+  "audit",
+  "approval readiness",
+  "approval readiness checks",
+  "approve this",
+  "pr review",
+  // Bug finding, regression hunting, and approval-readiness are explicit review asks per SKILL.md
+  "find bugs",
+  "bug finding",
+  "regression hunting",
+  "regression hunt",
+  "find regressions",
+];
+
+export const PLAN_KEYWORDS = [
+  "plan",
+  "design",
+  "architecture",
+  "architect",
+  "proposal",
+  "strategy",
+  "approach",
+];
+
+export const DEBUG_KEYWORDS = [
+  "debug",
+  "fix",
+  "failure",
+  "failing",
+  "error",
+  "crash",
+  "root cause",
+  "broken",
+  "investigate",
+];
+
+export const IMPLEMENT_KEYWORDS = [
+  "implement",
+  "build",
+  "create",
+  "write",
+  "refactor",
+  "add",
+  "change",
+  "update",
+  "edit",
+  "enhance",
+  "modify",
+  "adjust",
+  "improve",
+  "fix the code",
+  "fix the formatting",
+  "fix the logic",
+];
+

+  return includesAny(text, EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS);

+  return includesAny(text, TOOL_KEYWORDS);
+}
+
+export function isComplexOrchestration(text) {
+  return includesAny(text, ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS);
+}

## Routing Policy Source-of-Truth Refactor

### What changed

**New canonical module: `.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs`**

Moved all routing constants and pure helpers here — outside the extension directory. The key benefit: the eval harness renames `model-router/` → `model-router.eval-disabled/` when disabling the extension, which previously broke test imports. Since `lib/` is not in the disable-paths list, this module is always importable by both the extension and the test suite.

**`.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs`** → thin re-export  
Seven lines that just `export * from "../../lib/routing-policy.mjs"`. Backward-compatible for anything already importing from `./policy.mjs`.

**`.github/extensions/model-router/extension.mjs`** → updated import  
Changed `from "./policy.mjs"` → `from "../../lib/routing-policy.mjs"`. After the eval rename, `model-router.eval-disabled/extension.mjs` still resolves `../../lib/routing-policy.mjs` to `.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs` correctly.

**`tests/routing-policy.test.mjs`** (new, 35 tests)  
Imports from the stable `../.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs` path. Covers:
- `MODEL_CANDIDATES` structure and tier consistency (no `high` effort in economy, at least one `high` in reasoning, no duplicate `id+effort` pairs)
- `MODEL_FAMILIES` consistency with `MODEL_CANDIDATES`
- Keyword array exports (`TOOL_KEYWORDS`, `ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS`, `EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS`) — guards against accidental emptying
- `classifyPrompt` routing stability for representative inputs
- `getTierCandidates` ordering contracts per tier
- `dedupeCandidates` and `orderCandidates` review diversity
- `buildAdditionalContext` fallback-note regressions (exact match, same-family, cross-family)

**`.github/skills/model-router/references/routing-matrix.md`** → source annotations  
Added a callout at the top pointing to `routing-policy.mjs` as the source of truth. Filled in the complete `TOOL_KEYWORDS` list (was partially listed before — 5 of 16 keywords) and the `ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS` list, both with inline comments noting which export they come from.

**`README.md`** → updated all three references  
Changed "How It Works", "Model Coverage source-of-truth", "Files", and "Customization" sections to point to `.github/lib/routing-policy.mjs` instead of `policy.mjs`. Added `lib/routing-policy.mjs` to the Files tree.

### Runtime behavior
Unchanged — same functions, same data, same signatures. The only structural change is where the code lives.
